title: Trading Strategy Card Schema
description: Schema for documenting trading strategies with entry, exit, and confluence rules

entry_conditions.description: Conditions that must be met for entry
example: Price breaks above Asian high within first 30 mins of London open
No major economic events in next 2 hours
Take profit at daily resistance level
Time-based: close at 4:00 PM London time

stop_loss_logic.description: How to determine stop loss placement
stop_loss_logic.example: Place stop loss 10 pips below the Asian low or 1% of account risk, whichever is smaller

take_profit_logic.description: How to determine take profit levels
take_profit_logic.example: TP1 at recent daily resistance, TP2 at weekly resistance, TP3 trailing stop after TP1 hit

best_sessions.description: Best trading sessions for this strategy

confluence_factors.description: Factors that increase setup strength when aligned
example: Daily trend direction matches entry direction
Price near support/resistance that aligns with Fibonacci level

notes.example: Avoid this strategy 1 hour before major economic events. Works best on GBP/USD and EUR/GBP. Requires active monitoring.

example.name: Institutional Support Bounce
entry_conditions: Price breaks and retests a major support level (horizontal or trendline)
Retest occurs on reduced volume (smart money absorption)
Price bounces from support without breaking it
Entry on close above support with volume confirmation
exit_conditions: Close below support level on 4h timeframe
5-day time limit if no movement toward target
example.stop_loss_logic: Place stop loss 10-15 pips below the support level. If account is 10k and risk is 1%, stop loss cannot be more than 10 pips max.
example.take_profit_logic: TP1 at 50% of daily resistance zone, TP2 at full resistance zone, close 50% position at TP1
confluence_factors: Support level aligns with 0.618 Fibonacci retrace
Level has been retested 2+ times historically
Support is on a horizontal S/R, trendline, and moving average simultaneously
Daily trend is still bullish (higher lows)
example.notes: This is a mechanical strategy with high win rate but requires patience. Do not chase entries. Wait for price to come to support naturally. Works best in trending markets, avoid in ranging markets.

examples.name: 15m MA Crossover Scalp
entry_conditions: Fast MA (9) crosses above Slow MA (21)
Entry on candle close above both MAs
Minimum 10 pips from Asian session open
exit_conditions: Fast MA crosses below Slow MA
examples.take_profit_logic: Target: 15 pips. Move to breakeven after 10 pips profit.
examples.notes: Fast-paced, requires active monitoring. Use alerts. Best for EURUSD and GBPUSD. Max 3 trades per session.
